lines.line: # Introduction and Childhood Memories

> The interview begins with the interviewee describing a well-balanced childhood that included participation in church and community activities. They recall the connectedness of their family, their involvement with aunts and uncles, and spending holidays traveling across parts of the island.
** childhood, family, church, community, travel **

metadata.title: Introduction and Childhood Memories
metadata.synopsis: The interview begins with the interviewee describing a well-balanced childhood that included participation in church and community activities. They recall the connectedness of their family, their involvement with aunts and uncles, and spending holidays traveling across parts of the island.

lines.line: # Family Influence and Geographical Background

> The impact of extended family, especially grandparents living in the Bull Head Mountains. Christmas and Easter were spent adventurously with them, emphasizing the important role of family in the interviewee's life.
** family, grandparents, holidays, mountains, adventure **

metadata.title: Family Influence and Geographical Background
metadata.synopsis: The impact of extended family, especially grandparents living in the Bull Head Mountains. Christmas and Easter were spent adventurously with them, emphasizing the important role of family in the interviewee's life.

lines.line: # Immigrating to America

> The interviewee explains their move from Jamaica to America in 1988. They discuss the family dynamics that facilitated this move, including their mother's pursuit of a Green Card through family connections.
** immigration, America, Green Card, family, move **
keywords: ** immigration, America, Green Card, family, move **

metadata.title: Immigrating to America
metadata.synopsis: The interviewee explains their move from Jamaica to America in 1988. They discuss the family dynamics that facilitated this move, including their mother's pursuit of a Green Card through family connections.
metadata.keywords: immigration, America, Green Card, family, move

lines.line: # Educational Pursuits

> An overview of the interviewee's educational journey, including the initial decision to further education in the U.S. and the subsequent degrees obtained. They pursued studies in business and actuarial science.
** education, college, business administration, actuarial science **
keywords: ** education, college, business administration, actuarial science **

metadata.title: Educational Pursuits
metadata.synopsis: An overview of the interviewee's educational journey, including the initial decision to further education in the U.S. and the subsequent degrees obtained. They pursued studies in business and actuarial science.
metadata.keywords: education, college, business administration, actuarial science

lines.line: # Career Path in Actuarial Science

> Discussing working in insurance without completing actuarial exams and enjoyment in the field. The interviewee reflects on the irony of choosing a profession requiring extensive study despite disliking it.
** career, insurance, actuarial science, exams, work **
keywords: ** career, insurance, actuarial science, exams, work **

metadata.title: Career Path in Actuarial Science
metadata.synopsis: Discussing working in insurance without completing actuarial exams and enjoyment in the field. The interviewee reflects on the irony of choosing a profession requiring extensive study despite disliking it.
metadata.keywords: career, insurance, actuarial science, exams, work

lines.line: # Religious Upbringing and Personal Views

> The interviewee elaborates on their religious upbringing, emphasizing the influence of church and religious teachings on their values and principles, albeit with a rebellious stance toward routine religious activities.
** religion, church, upbringing, values, principles **

metadata.title: Religious Upbringing and Personal Views
metadata.synopsis: The interviewee elaborates on their religious upbringing, emphasizing the influence of church and religious teachings on their values and principles, albeit with a rebellious stance toward routine religious activities.

lines.line: # High School Experience in Jamaica
> Recounting time at a strict boarding school in Jamaica aimed at grooming 'young ladies.' The education system and the path to attending a distinguished grammar school are discussed.
** high school, Jamaica, education, boarding school, grammar school **
keywords: ** high school, Jamaica, education, boarding school, grammar school **

metadata.title: High School Experience in Jamaica
metadata.synopsis: Recounting time at a strict boarding school in Jamaica aimed at grooming 'young ladies.' The education system and the path to attending a distinguished grammar school are discussed.
metadata.keywords: high school, Jamaica, education, boarding school, grammar school

lines.line: # Ancestry and Familial Heritage
> The interviewee shares limited knowledge about their ancestry and the recent efforts to learn more. They talk about their grandparents' farming lifestyle and community support.
** ancestry, heritage, family history, farming, community **
keywords: ** ancestry, heritage, family history, farming, community **

metadata.synopsis: The interviewee shares limited knowledge about their ancestry and the recent efforts to learn more. They talk about their grandparents' farming lifestyle and community support.

lines.line: # Moving and Settling in the U.S.
> The transition from Jamaica to the U.S. and eventually settling in Connecticut. Cultural shifts and the social environment in the new locations are also discussed.
** settling, Connecticut, cultural shift, transition, Manchester **
keywords: ** settling, Connecticut, cultural shift, transition, Manchester **

metadata.title: Moving and Settling in the U.S.
metadata.synopsis: The transition from Jamaica to the U.S. and eventually settling in Connecticut. Cultural shifts and the social environment in the new locations are also discussed.
metadata.keywords: settling, Connecticut, cultural shift, transition, Manchester

lines.line: # Employment and Work Environment
> The changing landscape of employment in the U.S. and a move towards working remotely due to industry shifts. Insights into the evolution of work culture over the years.
** employment, work culture, remote work, insurance industry, changes **
keywords: ** employment, work culture, remote work, insurance industry, changes **

metadata.title: Employment and Work Environment
metadata.synopsis: The changing landscape of employment in the U.S. and a move towards working remotely due to industry shifts. Insights into the evolution of work culture over the years.

lines.line: # Community and Social Life

> Discussion on the sense of community in Bloomfield, ongoing involvement in activities like tennis, and observations about changes in local neighborhoods. Reflections on the social dynamics of Bloomfield are shared.
** community, social life, Bloomfield, activities, neighborhood **
keywords: ** community, social life, Bloomfield, activities, neighborhood **

metadata.title: Community and Social Life
metadata.synopsis: Discussion on the sense of community in Bloomfield, ongoing involvement in activities like tennis, and observations about changes in local neighborhoods. Reflections on the social dynamics of Bloomfield are shared.
metadata.keywords: community, social life, Bloomfield, activities, neighborhood

lines.line: # Reflections on Bloomfield and Conclusion

> The interview concludes with reflections on the charm and potential growth of Bloomfield. While considering possible changes, there is a sense of contentment with life in the town and thoughts on attending more community meetings.
** Bloomfield, reflections, community growth, contentment, future plans **
keywords: ** Bloomfield, reflections, community growth, contentment, future plans **

story.title: Opal Bravo Oral History 03172023.WAV
story.description: The interviewee reflects on life in Jamaica, highlighting a strong familial bond and community involvement. They discuss the move to the United States for educational opportunities, influenced by familial expectations of upward mobility. They pursued a career in actuarial science and finance, working primarily in the insurance industry. The transition to life in Connecticut contrasts the more communal atmosphere in Jamaica with the colder social interaction experienced in the U.S. Over time, the interviewee acclimated by actively engaging in local activities. They enjoy the calm of Bloomfield but are wary of overdevelopment affecting its charm. Jamaican roots remain significant, appreciated in cultural and culinary facets.
